questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt: The 'Falepili Union Treaty', recently seen in the news, is primarily associated with which of the following?

options.text: A strategic partnership between India and Nordic countries for green energy transition.
A bilateral agreement addressing climate-induced threats and security between Australia and a Pacific Island nation.
A coalition of Amazonian countries to halt oil drilling expansion in indigenous territories.
A United Nations framework to monitor global food systems and agricultural resilience in Africa.

questions.explanation: The 2023 Falepili Union Treaty is a bilateral agreement signed between Tuvalu and Australia. It was driven by the existential threat Tuvalu faces from rising sea levels due to climate change. Option A refers to the Green Strategic Partnership (India and Denmark). Option C relates to recent indigenous protests in Ecuador. Option D relates to the UN Food Systems Summit hosted by Ethiopia.
questions.takeaway: The Falepili Union Treaty is a landmark climate-security and migration agreement between Australia and Tuvalu, reflecting the severe impact of sea-level rise on Pacific Island nations.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Tuvalu: Signed the 2023 Falepili Union Treaty with Australia due to threats from rising sea levels.
